_doc: 2000-tick variant of the IO Profile Runner config. Tuned to produce a long trace with moderate per-tick activity: no single tick dominates, but every tick has visible signals on at least one gauge or event track. Use with: dotnet run -c Release -- ioprofile-2k.json. Output lands at ioprofile-2k.typhon-trace alongside the default-variant trace so both can be compared side-by-side.

database._doc: 128 MB cache — 4× the default variant. Rationale: at 32 MB (4096 × 8 KB pages) the spawnCheckpointLoop stage's ~250K Versioned entities would fill every slot dirty+epoch-protected and the engine would enter backpressure waits (observed: 3×5s waits between gcChurn and txFlood in earlier runs), pushing total runtime past a reasonable cap. 128 MB (16K pages) gives the workload enough headroom to run without stalling — cache churn is still plentiful (visible Disk Read/Write + PageEvicted activity throughout), just not pathologically starved.

workload._doc: Stage budget — total ~2001 ticks: 1 + 1 + 500 + 500 + 500 + 497 + 1 + 1 = 2001. Each non-one-shot stage runs one iteration per tick, producing a steady signal rather than bursts.

spawnBurst._comment: One-shot warm-up: 5K entities in the first tick. Seeds the page cache and creates a clear initial spike on every track.

spawnCheckpointLoop._comment: 500 iterations × 500 entities = 250K Versioned entities across 500 ticks. Per-tick cost ~9 ms. Produces sustained checkpoint + commit activity on the Transactions / Checkpoint mini-rows and keeps the PageCache Dirty bucket oscillating as checkpoints drain each tick's writes.

txFlood._comment: 500 ticks × 50 independent commits per tick = 25K commits. Drives the Tx+UoW gauge's per-tick commit/created rate lines to a sustained plateau visible across the middle third of the trace.

gcChurn._comment: 500 ticks × 8K Hashtable entries per tick. Tuned DOWN from the default variant's 40K — 500 ticks of even moderate pressure is plenty to show periodic Gen1/Gen2 collections without making any single tick's duration dominate the tick timeline.

transientChurn._comment: 497 × 80 = ~40K transient spawns across 497 ticks → ~560 component-segment pages, comfortably under the 1024-page ceiling imposed by the PagesPerBlock=256 workaround for Issue #244. The Transient Store gauge climbs monotonically through this stage and then flattens.

readBurst._comment: Single one-shot read pass at the end of the workload.

profile._doc: Identical to the default variant's opt-in set.
